name: Login / Sign-in Entry Point
description: Discoverable, working sign-in path from the main site for an existing customer to authenticate.

frontend.evidence: frontend/index.html:13154-13157 — the only 'Sign In' surface reachable from the homepage is a dead-end modal ('Sign In Required') whose only control is an OK button that removes the modal (onclick="document.getElementById('_monitor-modal')?.remove()"); it does not link anywhere. The real, working login form (#login-overlay, 'Sign in to your account') exists at frontend/user-dashboard.html:747, confirmed present and functional, but is reachable only via a small footer link, not from primary navigation. Both facts independently re-verified 2026-07-08 (in addition to docs/SAAS_PRODUCTIZATION_MISSION_BRIEF.md:202).

navigation.evidence: No header/primary-nav entry links to frontend/user-dashboard.html's login overlay; only a small dim footer link does.

test_coverage.evidence: workers/test/gdprAccountDeletion.test.mjs, workers/test/emailWiringTask3Phase1.test.mjs, and workers/test/phase10PasswordReset.test.mjs all import directly from workers/src/handlers/auth.js — confirmed by direct import-statement grep, 2026-07-08.

verification.evidence: workers/src/handlers/auth.js read directly (12 exported handle* functions confirmed); frontend/index.html:13148-13160 and frontend/user-dashboard.html:744-750 read directly and independently re-confirmed to match the described dead-end-modal / buried-form pattern.

contradicts_doc.claim: Auth: signup / login / session / logout / change-password | GA APPROVED | Live-verified incl. negative paths across Phases IX–X
contradicts_doc.correction: "Live-verified" here means the API endpoints were exercised directly (dynamic_api), not that a customer can find and use the login form from the production site. The homepage's only visible sign-in affordance is a dead-end modal; the real, working form is reachable only via a small footer link. Corrected status: NOT READY (P1 — this is a broken customer journey, not merely an undiscovered feature, because it is literally the entry point to every other capability on the platform).

notes: Classified P1 rather than P2 because this is the single entry point every other authenticated capability depends on — a customer who cannot find sign-in cannot reach anything else, regardless of how complete the rest of the platform is.
